name: data-pipeline
description: System2 overlay for data pipeline engineering with Apache Airflow — ELT/ETL jobs and the scheduling/delivery of labeled datasets for ML experimentation, validation, and evaluation cycles

advisory_sources.name: Airflow facts
advisory_sources.description: Authoritative Airflow-3/2.x, asset/dataset/watcher scheduling, and OpenLineage facts the orchestrator can consult inline during delegation. Resolved by orchestrator relay — no tool, MCP server, or permission required.

design_constraints.summary: Design DAGs and data deliverables to be idempotent and data-aware by default, scale ceremony to blast radius, keep tool choices advisory, and never attribute re-run safety to Airflow DAG-versioning (it is engineered via idempotency).

required_output_sections.summary: For shared, canonical, or production deliverables, require per-deliverable design sections: idempotency/backfill, orchestration-vs-compute boundary, layered data quality, lineage/provenance, and (ML) splits, point-in-time correctness, eval/golden set, and reproducibility.

guardrails.summary: Write Airflow requirements in the Airflow-3 idiom with explicit 2.x notes, keep named tools advisory, never claim a tool eliminates a failure mode, default data-quality to native SQL check operators, and prefer open-source options.

required_req_sections.summary: Require idempotency/backfill requirements and, for ML deliverables, split, point-in-time, eval/golden, and versioning requirement sections — tier-scoped to the deliverable's blast radius.

implementation_discipline.summary: Implement idempotent tasks (templated intervals, overwrite-by-partition, no blind append), offload heavy compute to engines, use native SQL check operators by default, use the no-execution-date paradigm for non-interval ML DAGs, and do not treat a feature store as a train/serving skew guarantee.

verification_rules.summary: After implementing, self-check that no time-dependent or heavy work runs at DAG parse time, that OpenLineage emission is present, and that training joins are point-in-time.

verification_workflow.summary: Add severity-tagged advisory checks (info/warn/block-candidate) for parse-time hygiene, layered DQ presence, lineage emission, and — for ML — no train/eval leakage, point-in-time joins, and explicitly tested train/serving skew (not assumed from a feature store). Each finding uses the {check_id, severity, location, rationale, fix_hint} shape.

test_authoring_rules.summary: Author tests that assert idempotent re-runs, leak-safe splits, eval-set version and provenance, and dataset reproducibility/identifiability.

review_criteria.summary: Review for in-task heavy compute, missing provenance/lineage, DAG-versioning-as-replay claims, blanket GE/Soda/dbt data-quality framing, and feature-store-prevents-skew overclaims — each treated as non-conformant.

surface_area_delta.summary: Track the governance surface a change introduces: new sources of truth versus reuse of canonical datasets, and medallion/semantic-layer layering — tier-scoped to blast radius.

threat_model_scope.summary: Add data-pipeline threat dimensions: secrets in DAG code versus a secrets backend, PII leaking into lineage or logs, and untrusted source data at ingestion boundaries.

required_sections.description: State the re-run and backfill strategy: templated execution intervals plus overwrite-by-partition so re-runs are safe. Re-run safety is an engineered property (idempotent, retry-safe tasks), not a consequence of Airflow DAG-versioning.
Define what the DAG coordinates versus what an engine computes: Airflow schedules, validates, and emits lineage; heavy or large-scale compute is pushed to dbt/Spark/the warehouse.

Define data-quality checks layered through the pipeline rather than one terminal gate. Native SQL check operators are the default approach; third-party frameworks (Great Expectations, Soda, dbt tests) are situational, not blanket requirements.
Require OpenLineage run/dataset emission and an artifact-level provenance stamp (sources, queries, versions) on delivered outputs, without logging raw sensitive payloads.
Define train/validation/test (and optional holdout) split discipline and point-in-time correctness of feature/label joins as the core defense against leakage.
Define the golden/eval set: its definition, versioning, provenance, and human-review path. Delivery cadence and any registry pattern are advisory and may be marked TBD.

Define how datasets are versioned and reproduced. Candidate tools (e.g., DVC, LakeFS, Delta/Iceberg time-travel) are advisory open-source options, none mandated; an optional model/dataset registry link is TBD.
Define an explicit test plan for leakage and train/serving skew. Skew must be tested; a feature store is noted as an aid for consistency, never as a guarantee that prevents skew.

auxiliary_agents.name: dp-pipeline-scout
auxiliary_agents.role: Optional read-only fact-gatherer for Airflow, asset/dataset scheduling, and OpenLineage facts. Returns compact structured summaries; advisory only; never blocks; cannot spawn subagents.
